title: Why Sales Make You Spend $800 More Every Year
hook: You see 40% off and feel like you're winning — but researchers found sale shoppers spend more, not less.
angle: Counterintuitive finding that discounts trigger unplanned spending via dopamine — backed by purchase behavior data and retailer price-inflation tactics.
audience: Anyone who hunts deals, uses coupons, or thinks they're 'good at sales' — shoppers who believe they save but actually overspend.
key_numbers: Retailers inflate prices up to 83% before marking them down
